description: Four photos of a KBG lawn in Columbus OH with multiple concurrent problems: (1) a circular brown-patch fungal ring of dead straw-colored turf surrounded by healthy green, (2) a wide view of dollar-spot infection with numerous bleached/tan patches scattered across the canopy, (3) chinch-bug damage producing patchy yellowing and dying areas with healthy turf interspersed, (4) a dead/bare strip of turf along a hardscape edge representing bare_spots. Tests the AI's ability to synthesize across multiple distinct issue types — fungal disease (two presentations), insect damage, and bare areas — and prioritize them correctly.

photos.caption: Circular brown-patch fungal ring of dead straw-colored turf surrounded by healthy green KBG — textbook Rhizoctonia brown patch presentation.
Wide view of dollar-spot infection on KBG showing numerous small bleached/tan circular patches scattered across the canopy — classic Clarireedia damage pattern.
Macro / extension identification photo showing multiple insect pests (chinch-bug nymphs) on a grass blade — direct visual confirmation of pest pressure in the lawn.
Dead/bare strip of turf along a hardscape edge — the unrecovered area where the canopy has died and bare soil/dead matter is visible adjacent to healthy turf.

profile.notes: Lawn has multiple issues this year: some circular brown/tan patches, bare spots in both front and back, and what looks like insect feeding damage at the edges of dying areas. Really struggling to figure out what is going on.
profile.currentRoutine: Mow at 3 inches, water 2-3x per week. No pesticide or fungicide applied this season.

groundTruth.photoNotes: Four photos showing a KBG lawn with three distinct concurrent issues. Photo 1 shows a textbook circular brown-patch fungal ring (Rhizoctonia). Photo 2 shows widespread dollar-spot infection (Clarireedia) — multiple small bleached patches. Photo 3 shows chinch-bug-driven patchy yellowing/dying. Photo 4 shows a dead/bare strip of turf along a hardscape edge (bare_spots). Cross-photo synthesis required to triage all three issue types. The lawn is materially compromised by visible damage across all four photos, so healthScore should land in 40-65 reflecting multiple active stressors.
